items.summary: Fix stat persistence during character creation: when the player allocates 8 stat points and confirms creation, write the allocated Strength, Dexterity, Intelligence, and Vitality values directly into the character's baseStats record in stable storage. Do not use a temporary UI-only state object that gets discarded. Ensure no post-creation initialization step overwrites the assigned values with defaults.

Fix the ability point exploit and rework the ability system: characters start with exactly 1 ability point; earn +1 ability point every 10 levels (so at level 100 a character has 11 total points); each ability purchase costs 1 ability point; block the purchase UI when availableAbilityPoints == 0; enforce a hard cap of 3 simultaneously equipped abilities (players may own more than 3 but only equip 3); expand the ability pool in abilities.ts to approximately 15 abilities spread across melee, ranged, magic, and tank archetypes.

Rebalance monster difficulty: reduce the base damage of all monsters by approximately 10–15% and reduce HP scaling per dungeon level by approximately 10%. Apply these reductions in the monster templates in monsters.ts and in any scaling logic in combatEngine.ts. Keep encounters challenging but smooth out the early progression curve.

Restrict the random item affix pool in lootGenerator.ts to only the following valid stats: Strength, Dexterity, Intelligence, Vitality, +HP, +Physical Damage, +Magic Damage, +Defense, +Critical Chance. Remove all other affix types that are currently generated (e.g. any regen, speed, or unrecognized stats). Apply this restriction to all item types: weapons, armor, and charms/trinkets.

Reduce the global item drop rate by 50% in lootGenerator.ts. This applies to all dungeon modes (Catacombs, Depths, Ascension Trial) and all monster types. If drops are determined by a probability roll, halve that probability. If drops are determined by a count, halve the expected count.

Completely remove the gold system from the entire codebase: (a) backend — remove all gold fields from character/player records in main.mo, remove any gold rewards in dungeon result processing, remove gold from death penalty logic; (b) frontend — remove gold display from CharacterSheet, Navigation, and any other screen; remove gold reward lines from combat/loot output; update the Softcore death penalty to apply XP loss only (no gold loss); remove any vendor or store UI that requires gold. Gear must be self-found or traded via the marketplace only.
